purpose: Translates the maturity-dependent correlation bifurcation into actionable portfolio construction: which maturities hedge equities vs which add correlated risk
components: 2-5Y as equity hedge (negative stock-bond correlation, cyclically responsive)
10-30Y as separate correlated risk factor (positive correlation, fiscal supply dynamics)
Term premium level as regime indicator (transitional zone at 50-100bp)
MOVE regime for bifurcation stability assessment
RSP vs SPY as trade expression for term premium shock positioning
current_assessment: Bifurcation is currently operative and has been confirmed across 6 iterations by 8 agents. Term premium at 50-100bp is in the inherently unstable transitional zone with $15-20T+ in strategies calibrated to the prior negative-correlation regime. Only 3 historical transitions exist with different macro contexts.
limitations: Only 3 historical correlation regime transitions to calibrate from
Transitional zone (50-100bp term premium) is inherently unstable
Could resolve via policy intervention within 3-14 months per KB precedents
Equal-weighted vs cap-weighted ERP gap (200bp) complicates index-level positioning
Factor rotation prescriptions carry false precision at sector level
